data.title: Appeals court denies effort to redo criminal case against Boeing
data.description:
A federal appeals court on Tuesday tossed out an effort to revive a criminal case against Boeing (BA) over two deadly crashes of 737 Max jets, Bloomberg Law reported. The court rejected a claim by victims’ families that the federal government formed an improper settlement with the aviation giant last year.

A three-judge panel of the 5th US Circuit Court of Appeals in New Orleans determined the complainants didn’t have the legal standing to challenge a deal between Boeing (BA) and the U.S. Justice Department. A federal judge in Texas approved the settlement.

The ruling maintains a $1.1 billion settlement with federal prosecutors that lets Boeing (BA) avoid a criminal conspiracy charge in relation to the crashes of Lion Air Flight 610 in October 2018 and Ethiopian Airlines Flight 302 in March 2019. The accidents killed 349 people and were linked to faulty software.

data.title: Aluminum poised for biggest monthly gain in eight years as Iran war disrupts supplies
data.description:
Aluminum futures on the London Metal Exchange are headed for their biggest monthly gain since April 2018, as the Middle East war disrupted supplies and damaged local production facilities, tightening the global market.

The metal (LMAHDS03:COM) exceeded $3,500/ton Tuesday on the LME and is on course for a 12%-plus monthly gain, bucking a broader downtrend for metals in March.

Some 9% of global ​aluminum supply comes from the Persian Gulf, and most companies there have been unable to export the metal beyond the region since Iran effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz, which has forced customers to use their inventories, leaving the market with little buffer to cushion any supply shocks.

Over the weekend, Iranian drones and missiles struck plants run by Aluminium Bahrain and Emirates Global Aluminium; the damaged smelter at EGA's Al-Taweelah plant produced 1.6M tons of cast metal in 2025.

Output from Al-Taweelah can be "written off" for the long term, which could flip the market from a supply surplus of 200K tons to a deficit of 1.3M tons next year, Natixis analyst Bernard Dahdah said in a note.

Alcoa (AA) jumped 5.8% in early trading Tuesday, as Jefferies analyst Christopher LaFemina said meaningful earnings upgrades could be coming for the company as aluminum supply disruptions in the Middle East lift metal prices.

"With obvious challenges in shipping alumina through the Strait of Hormuz, additional supply shocks in aluminum could lead to fly-up price spikes," LaFemina said in a note, tapping Alcoa (AA) as the biggest beneficiary within his coverage, although the shares are not immune from recession risk and operating costs are likely to rise given the jump in energy prices.

data.title: Lululemon's founder keeps calling for a return to the company's yoga pants roots
data.description:
Lululemon (LULU) founder Chip Wilson continues to pound away at the company's current management team. In a new letter issued to shareholders, Wilson asked for support for the campaign to implement board changes and new leadership at the company.

Wilson and his allies say Lululemon (LULU) has drifted away from the creative, brand-driven company they believe he built, and they want shareholders to support a fresh slate of nominees for the board. The sharp share price decline on the days of the Breezethrough recall and Gte Loe recall were highlighted in the below graphic.

 

 

 

 

The letter also indicated that the activist group plans to use a universal proxy card, which lets shareholders mix and match candidates from both sides instead of choosing only one full slate.

Chip Wilson started the company in the late 1990s after previously building another apparel business called Westbeach. He helped turn Lululemon into a major athletic apparel brand. He served as CEO until 2005, helped take the company public in 2007, and later held other leadership roles before eventually stepping away from any management role in 2015. Since then, he has publicly argued that Lululemon (LULU) has become too corporate and lost some of the originality that made it successful, which is why he is leading the broad effort to push for change.

data.title: Elon Musk denies excluding Robinhood, SoFi from SpaceX IPO
data.description:
Elon Musk has denied excluding Robinhood Markets (HOOD) and SoFi Technologies (SOFI) from the SpaceX (SPACE) IPO, according to Musk's X post.

Robinhood and SoFi had pitched for the small retail role in the IPO, but SpaceX was reportedly considering cutting them out.

"These reports are false," Musk said in response to an X post by the Tesla Club—Tesla Owners Silicon Valley. Tesla Owners Silicon Valley had asked Musk to include Robinhood in the SpaceX IPO.

Musk was said to be talking about allocating as much as 30% of SpaceX's initial public offering to individual investors, at least three times as much as in a typical IPO.

Morgan Stanley's (MS) E*Trade was said to be in talks to take the leading role for smaller U.S. investors in the IPO.

data.title: Job openings continue to dwindle in February: JOLTS report
data.description:
The number of job openings in the U.S. fell to 6.882M in Feb from 7.240M in January (revised from 6.946M), according to data released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on Monday. That's almost in-line when compared with the 6.890M consensus.

In February, the number of job openings decreased in accommodation and food services (-211,000) and in mining and logging (-12,000)

The job openings rate slipped to 4.2% in February from 4.4% in January.

The number of layoffs and discharges remained unchanged at 1.7 million. The layoffs and discharges rate was little changed at 1.1 percent. The number of layoffs and discharges increased in retail trade (+72,000). Layoffs and discharges decreased in nondurable goods manufacturing (-26,000) and in federal government (-3,000).

 

The quits rate were little changed at 1.9%.

The number of hires decreased to 4.8 million (-498,000) in February and was down by 387,000 over the year. The hires rate decreased over the month to 3.1 percent. This was the lowest hires rate since April 2020 when it was also 3.1 percent. In February, the number of hires decreased in accommodation and food services (-178,000) and in construction (-88,000).

data.title: CarGurus is the best performing communication services stock in March
data.description:
U.S. communication services stocks have weathered a turbulent month, declining around 7% through late March as Middle East war escalation fueled inflation worries and a stronger dollar.

Below is a list of the top 10 communication services stocks based on their price performance over March. The list includes companies filtered by sector and market capitalization data, ranked from highest to lowest one-month performance.

The list is topped by CarGurus, Inc. (CARG), with an impressive one-month performance of 14.92%. Iridium Communications Inc. (IRDM) follows with an 11.94% gain, while Grindr Inc. (GRND) rounds out the top three with an 8.35% increase.

In the middle of the pack, Versant Media Group, Inc. (VSNT) and Pinterest, Inc. (PINS) posted gains of 6.81% and 5.14%, respectively. Most stocks on the list currently carry a Hold Quant Rating, including The New York Times Company (NYT) with a rating of 3.34 and Fox Corporation (FOXA) at 3.16.

Here is the list:

  1. CarGurus, Inc. (CARG), 1 month performance percentage: 14.92%

  2. Iridium Communications Inc. (IRDM), 1 month performance percentage: 11.94%

  3. Grindr Inc. (GRND), 1 month performance percentage: 8.35%

  4. Versant Media Group, Inc. (VSNT), 1 month performance percentage: 6.81%

  5. Pinterest, Inc. (PINS), 1 month performance percentage: 5.14%

  6. Fox Corporation (FOXA), 1 month performance percentage: 4.95%

  7. Lionsgate Studios Corp. (LION), 1 month performance percentage: 4.44%

  8. The New York Times Company (NYT), 1 month performance percentage: 4.34%

  9. Sirius XM Holdings Inc. (SIRI), 1 month performance percentage: 3.64%

  10. IAC Inc. (IAC), 1 month performance percentage: 2.09%

Communication Services ETFs: (XLC), (VOX), (IYZ), (RSPC), and (XTL)
